column1: Juan Soto stands out as the top pick, offering a strong combination of reliability and a favorable platoon advantage against Aaron Nola, with a hit in over 70% of his games this season. Carson Benge also brings a lefty bat atop the order and is riding a recent hot stretch, making him a solid secondary choice. Brandon Marsh deserves a quick look for his steady contact skills and another lefty matchup, but Soto’s consistency makes him the clear leader for Beat The Streak.
